name: Indian Chicken Curry Coca-Cola
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Curry powder (or more for experienced palates)
1 cup Coffee cream or undiluted Evaporated milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken pieces.
2. In a pot of boiling salted water, cook the chicken with a few celery tops. Cover and simmer about 1 hour or until fork-tender. Drain and strain the broth; reserve.
3. Bone the chicken and cut it into 1/2-inch pieces to measure about 2-1/2 cups.
4. In a skillet, melt the butter/margarine. Add the apple, onion, and curry powder and sauté for 5 minutes, stirring well.
5. Stir in the raisins, 1 cup of the reserved chicken broth, and the COCA-COLA. Cook until simmering gently.
6. In a bowl, mix flour with the coffee cream/evaporated milk, stirring until smooth. Add this mixture to the onion/apple mixture along with salt and white pepper. Stir and cook over low heat until thick and creamy. Taste for seasoning.
7. Add the chicken and transfer to a covered container. Chill overnight.
8. Reheat in the top of a double boiler over hot water and serve on cooked rice with a selection of condiments.
NOTE: Provide a sampling of the following condiments for sprinkling on top of each serving: grated coconut, chopped peanuts, chopped raw onions, raisins, sweet pickle relish, chutney, chow chow, and/or lime wedges.
